title: Clean Environment Reproduction

defects.finding: No dedicated gate proved that the exact candidate could reproduce from clean checkouts without inherited repository artifacts.
defects.closure: Added two independent Linux exact-head checkouts, fail-closed residue inspection and a complete repository CI run in each isolated home.

defects.finding: Environment evidence did not explicitly reject user-site packages, external cache paths or pre-existing sandbox files.
defects.closure: Added a standard-library preflight that requires isolated profile, temp and cache paths, disables user-site and bytecode writes, rejects generated checkout residue and emits machine-readable evidence.

defects.finding: Windows reproduction relied on hosted-runner freshness without binding HOME, USERPROFILE, LOCALAPPDATA, APPDATA, TEMP, TMP and pip cache to one explicit clean-room root.
defects.closure: The M8 Windows job now creates an empty ephemeral profile tree, binds every relevant path to it, installs the hash-locked Windows dependency without cache reuse and records preflight evidence.

defects.finding: Clean-source reproduction and the native one-button Windows path were not enforced together by one milestone gate.
defects.closure: The exact-head Windows clean-room job runs M8 contracts and the existing native one-button Red Team, which includes installer build, fresh install, repair, contamination rejection, complete XLSX path and full repository CI.

defects.finding: A clean-environment result could be misattributed if checkout identity and post-run source immutability were not asserted.
defects.closure: Every job asserts the event exact head; Linux runs require a clean checkout after CI, and Windows requires no tracked-file modification after native execution.

defects.finding: The first clean-room orchestration treated expected compileall bytecode residue as source contamination, and its first cleanup revision used an invalid indented Bash heredoc.
defects.closure: The gate now removes only explicitly recognized generated residue through a syntax-validated command, then requires a clean Git status. Both defects were confined to the M8 workflow and did not change product runtime behavior.

limitations: Hosted clean runners are reproducible CI environments, not a physical end-user workstation certification.
Browser-render screenshot evidence is outside M8 scope.
Production release, marketplace writes and final scoring remain blocked.
